channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: There are 3 large street lights not working along prioress street - causing safety issues, 3rd March
item.description:
Three council lights are not working along prioress street (SE1 4TE) London . Please can someone come and fix the bulbs- as it’s causing real Safety concerns for anyone living on this street/ but also pedestrians.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Prioress Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole exposed outside 1b, 3rd March
item.description:
Pothole exposed now outside 1b Elm Road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elm Road, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole increases by the day, 3rd March
item.description:
This pothole appears every year and despite repeated reporting this year no repairs actions. It is heavily used being a bus route for 8 buses serving the area and lorries.The speed limit camera has been removed due to major road work going on this stretch of road and strong and continuous vibrations is felt with each and every speeding buses over the pothole. All of which is unnerving and effecting sleep. Urgent repairs are needed please.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Streatham Hill, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Yellow Barriers, 3rd March
item.description:
About 4/5 large plastic yellow work site barriers seem to have been left around this location after works (?). They are propped up by a street light column, or they were, and have been there for some time now. Clearly forgotten.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elmerside Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shopping Trolley, 3rd March
item.description:
1 shopping trolley sitting in this area. Possibly the same one I reported recently?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 233-235, High Street, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip, 3rd March
item.description:
Large furniture flytip around this location. Looks like sections of a bed. This seems to be a popular spot for flytip, dare I say the offenders are potentially residents in the houses nearby? Maybe a hidden camera would catch them.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 291, Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 3rd March
item.description:
Black graffiti on the traffic island bollards at this location.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oakwood Avenue, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins Litter Issue & location, 3rd March
item.description:
Please see picture attached. Could this bin situation be looked into please. As you can see, the bins are overflowing badly with litter falling on the floor. This in turn often spreads into the street. This whole area is pretty disgusting, attracts rodents, people abuse it, use it as a toilet, and needs attention badly (I realise that's down to whoever owns the block). But the other issue is, these bins aren't in their correct place. They should be around the corner out of sight within The Mews area (like they used to be). I don't think the binmen are putting them back correctly (which happens A LOT on residential streets). Can these bins be put back in their correct area, out of sight, around the corner in The Mews. This would stop litter build up and people abusing the area. I'm sure residents would appreciate this too.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 42A, High Street, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lamps out, 3rd March
item.description:
3 or 4 street lamps not working

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pettman Crescent, Plumstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Basketball sized pothole, 3rd March
item.description:
Its almost impossible to avoid this pothole right on the left turn onto long lane, your right tires will hit it
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Church Lane, Finchley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Severe cracked, damaged, loose road surface, 3rd March
item.description:
Huge breakage of road surface. Potholes, loose gravel/road. Difficult to traverse at all on a bike, let alone to traverse safely.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 16-18, Goswell Road, City of London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bin overflowing with rubbish, 3rd March
item.description:
I have reported this issue before. This bin was left here over Xmas. You have taken no action for over 2 months. Please could this matter now be resolved as a matter of urgency.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leroy Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parked on zigzag, 3rd March
item.description:
Outside 23 Camomile Street, EC3A 7LL, at the junction with Bishopsgate. Vehicle parked on pedestrian crossing zig‑zag lines MERCEDES-BENZ GY20JUT black Model E E 300 DE AMG LINE PREMIUM

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 133, Houndsditch, City of London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Missing rubbish bin, 2nd March
item.description:
The bus stop on Honor Oak Park at the junction of Lessing St always had a waste bin. Since Covid this hasn’t been replaced. I live at 23c Honor Oak Park SE23 1DZ where the bus stop is situated. We are constantly having to pick up rubbish discarded at the bus stop in order to keep the bus stop area clean and tidy, and put the rubbish in our own bins. People just think it’s ok to leave takeaway food packaging, and food, on the floor around the bus stop, not realising that foxes then come and rip it apart leaving the whole bus stop area in a mess for commuters te next morning. When there was a council bin situated here, we never saw this problem.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 27, Honor Oak Park, Honor Oak, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 2nd March
item.description:
Fly tipping!!!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Carronade Place, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: SORN car dumped in front of 2 Macallister House on Barnfield Estate, 2nd March
item.description:
Yet another long standing abandoned SORN car dumped on the Barnfield estate taking up spaces for residents.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wrottesley Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Human feaces, 2nd March

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 22-23, Neville Close, Peckham, Greater London

item.title: Litter on the bank outside Gail’s etc, 2nd March
item.description:
There is all sorts of,litter on the shrubby bank between the road and the raised walkway between Gail’s and Jumping Bean 41 High Street.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 66, High Street, Beckenham, Greater London
